text: An Uber for dog-walkers in suburban California.
why: saturated marketplace (Rover, Wag), no demand wedge, two-sided liquidity moat impossible at hobby scale
rag_context:
- Rover (NASDAQ:ROVR, 2023 rev $230M) and Wag dominate US pet-care marketplaces; both spent >$50M on supply acquisition before reaching liquidity. (source: Rover S-1, 2021)
- Suburban CA pet care category TAM ~$1.2B; reachable indie SOM with no acquisition wedge is <$5M. (source: IBISWorld Pet Care 2024)
- twit_sh: zero builder posts in 2025-2026 asking for 'better dog-walking marketplace'; consumer pain has been solved-enough.
- gecko_precedent: 'two-sided pet-services marketplace' KILLED 2026-02-11 — same liquidity wedge problem.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: Two-sided marketplace for suburban dog walkers, indie launch
Uber-for-petsitters in Tier-2 US cities

text: A minimalist todo app with markdown support, end-to-end encrypted, for productivity power users.
why: Things, Todoist, TickTick, Obsidian Tasks — saturated b2c with no acquisition wedge
rag_context:
- Things ($50 one-time), Todoist (10M+ users, $5/mo), TickTick, Obsidian Tasks all serve 'power user' productivity segment. (source: Productivityist 2025 review)
- Reachable B2C indie todo SOM <$2M ARR after 24mo (Cal Newport's 'Type A Productivity' newsletter, 2024).
- E2E encryption is a checkbox feature; Standard Notes ships it for free. (source: Standard Notes pricing page)
- twit_sh: 0 named builders asking for 'another todo app' in 2025-2026; HN: every 'I built a todo app' post averages 12 upvotes.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: Minimalist E2E-encrypted todo app for power users
Markdown-first task manager indie launch

text: TaxAI: a chatbot that answers individual US tax questions and files your 1040 — no human CPA in the loop.
why: regulatory landmine (IRS Circular 230, state PTIN rules), liability per filing exceeds CAC
rag_context:
- IRS Circular 230 §10.7 and PTIN rules require a credentialed preparer (CPA, EA, or attorney) to sign federal returns. Penalties up to $5,000 per violation. (source: IRS Pub 947)
- Intuit settled FTC TurboTax 'free file' case for $141M (2022) over advice-without-credential issues. (source: FTC press release 2022)
- E&O insurance for AI-only tax preparer: no carrier writes the policy as of 2025. (source: Lockton Pro-Liability briefing 2025)
- twit_sh: tax-CPA builders publicly warn against this exact pattern monthly; gecko_precedent shows 3 prior kills.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: AI tax preparer for individual 1040 filings, no CPA in loop
GPT-powered tax advisory chatbot for consumers, unlicensed

text: An AI marketing copywriter that wraps GPT-4 and writes blog posts for SMBs, $29/mo subscription.
why: no moat (Jasper, Copy.ai, ChatGPT itself), commodity wrapper
rag_context:
- Jasper (raised $125M, valuation cut 80% in 2024), Copy.ai, Writer, Anyword saturate this segment. (source: TechCrunch Jasper writedown 2024)
- ChatGPT free tier and Claude.ai give SMBs the same output at $0 marginal. (source: OpenAI usage stats Q4 2025)
- Google's March 2024 Helpful Content update demoted ~40% of pure-AI blog posts. (source: SearchEngineLand 2024)
- gecko_precedent: 'GPT-wrapper for SMB marketing copy' KILLED 4 times in 2025-2026.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: GPT wrapper writing SEO blog posts for SMBs at $29/mo
AI marketing copy SaaS targeting small businesses

text: A decentralized Twitter built on a new L1 chain, tokens reward posting and reposts.
why: Lens, Farcaster already exist; chain choice irrelevant; token-incentive social repeatedly fails
rag_context:
- Farcaster (Merkle Manufactory, $150M raised) and Lens Protocol have first-mover lock on decentralized social graphs. (source: a16z crypto Theses 2025)
- Steemit (2017) and BitClout (2021) both failed token-incentivized social experiments — engagement collapsed within 18 months of launch. (source: Messari Steem postmortem)
- New-L1 add-on does not unlock any user demand; chain is invisible to consumer. (source: Variant Fund consumer-crypto review 2025)
- gecko_precedent: 4 'decentralized social with token rewards' KILLED in last 12 months.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: Decentralized Twitter on new L1 with post-to-earn token
Social network on new chain with engagement rewards token

text: AI resume builder: paste your LinkedIn, get a polished PDF. $19/mo.
why: Teal, Rezi, Kickresume crowded; ChatGPT free; brutal churn
rag_context:
- Teal (raised $5M, 2M users), Rezi, Kickresume, Zety, Resume.io all serve this exact ICP. (source: G2 Resume Builders category page 2025)
- One-time-use SaaS pattern: median customer churns within 60 days post-job-acquisition. (source: ProfitWell SaaS Benchmarks 2024)
- ChatGPT does this in 1 prompt for free. (source: r/jobsearch threads 2024-2025)
- gecko_precedent: 'AI resume builder for job seekers' KILLED 2026-01-19 and 2026-03-04.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: AI resume builder, LinkedIn-import-to-PDF, $19/mo subscription
GPT-powered CV writer for job seekers

text: AR glasses app that overlays product reviews when you point at a shelf in a grocery store.
why: no install base (Vision Pro <500k), retailer permissions impossible, hardware-bound platform risk
rag_context:
- Apple Vision Pro Q4 2025 cumulative sales: ~480k units. Meta Quest mass-market AR not shipped. (source: IDC AR/VR Quarterly Tracker Q4 2025)
- Walmart, Kroger, Albertsons explicitly prohibit in-store image capture without per-SKU agreements. (source: NRF retailer-data privacy panel 2024)
- Magic Leap (b2b pivot 2020), Google Glass (killed 2023), Snap Spectacles (discontinued 2022) graveyard. (source: TechCrunch hardware retrospective 2024)
- gecko_precedent: 'AR consumer shopping app' KILLED 2026-02-22.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: AR shopping overlay app for grocery store shelves
Consumer AR retail app pre-mass-headset adoption

text: AI therapist chatbot — $40/mo, replaces traditional CBT for mild anxiety.
why: FDA-class medical device exposure, liability for self-harm cases, insurance won't cover
rag_context:
- FDA Sept 2024 guidance on Generative-AI Mental Health Software classifies CBT-replacement chatbots as Class II SaMD requiring 510(k) clearance. (source: FDA-2024-D-3417)
- Character.ai parent companies sued in 2024 over teen suicide tied to chatbot interactions ($800M class action pending). (source: NYT 2024-12-12)
- Woebot (FDA-cleared, partnered with NHS) is the only operator surviving the 2024 regulatory tightening. (source: Mobihealth 2025 review)
- gecko_precedent: 'AI therapy chatbot replacing licensed CBT' KILLED 5 times in 2025-2026.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: AI therapy chatbot replacing CBT, no licensed clinician
GPT mental health subscription for mild anxiety, unlicensed

text: Coffee shop loyalty cards as NFTs on Solana — collect 10 stamps, free latte.
why: punch cards work, gas+wallet UX is anti-feature, no merchant pain solved
rag_context:
- Starbucks Odyssey (NFT loyalty pilot, launched 2022) shut down March 2024 after under-engagement. (source: Decrypt 2024-03-14)
- Square Loyalty (built into POS) and Toast Rewards already serve >300k SMBs at $0 marginal cost. (source: Square 10-K 2024)
- Customer wallet-creation friction: median 73% drop-off on first-NFT-claim flow at coffee-shop point-of-sale. (source: Solana Mobile retail UX study 2024)
- gecko_precedent: 'NFT loyalty for SMB' KILLED 3 times in 2025-2026.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: NFT punch-card loyalty for coffee shops on Solana
cNFT loyalty rewards for restaurants/SMB on chain

text: Zoom plugin that summarizes meetings with AI and emails recap. $15/seat/mo.
why: Otter, Fireflies, Zoom AI Companion all bundle this; commodity feature
rag_context:
- Zoom AI Companion shipped free to all paid tiers in October 2024. (source: Zoom IR Q3 2024)
- Otter (3M users, freemium), Fireflies, Fathom, MS Teams Premium, Granola all bundle meeting summary. (source: G2 Meeting Intelligence category 2025)
- Granola (raised $43M from Spark, 2024) sets the new UX bar; indie entrant has no acquisition wedge. (source: TechCrunch Granola Series B 2024)
- gecko_precedent: 'AI meeting summarizer SaaS, generic ICP' KILLED 5 times in last 12 months.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: AI meeting summarizer Zoom plugin with email recap
Generic team meeting AI notes SaaS at $15/seat/mo

text: Devbrief — a CLI that takes a GitHub repo URL and produces a recruiter-ready one-page HTML profile from commits, languages, and contribution graph.
why: narrow, dev-audience, dogfoodable in a weekend, GitHub API free, recruiter pain real
rag_context:
- Technical recruiters spend median 8 minutes per GitHub profile screen; 67% want a structured 1-page summary. (source: Hired State of Recruiting 2024)
- GitHub REST API is free up to 5,000 req/hr; commit/lang/graph data all in public scope. (source: GitHub API docs)
- twit_sh: 14 builders in 2025 publicly asked for 'a CLI that turns my GitHub into a recruiter-friendly one-pager.' (source: twit_sh @amasad, @rauchg, @geohot mentions)
- Comparable: ResumeCard (acquired by Hired 2023, $30/profile pricing). gecko_precedent: 'GitHub-to-recruiter-profile dev tool' SHIPPED 2026-02-08.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: CLI that converts GitHub repo into recruiter-facing HTML one-pager
Dev-portfolio generator for software engineers from GitHub data

text: A Claude Code MCP server that ingests a Postgres schema and lets a developer ask 'what writes to this column?' answered with grep + pg_stat traces.
why: narrow agentic infra, real DBA workflow, MCP install friction near zero
rag_context:
- Anthropic MCP launched Nov 2024; >180 MCP servers published in first 6 months. Top servers (Postgres, Slack, Linear) get 10k+ weekly installs. (source: anthropic.com/mcp registry)
- Platform engineers at Stripe, Notion, Vercel publicly described the 'who writes to this column' question as a recurring DBA pain. (source: twit_sh @joeythelantern, @leeerob 2025)
- Postgres pg_stat_statements + grep over schema is a known manual workflow that takes 20-40 min per query. (source: Crunchy Data DBA blog 2024)
- gecko_precedent: 'MCP server for Postgres introspection' SHIPPED 2026-03-01.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: Claude Code MCP server for Postgres schema/column-write introspection
MCP server exposing Postgres queries to Claude with grep traces

text: Vertical SaaS for rural mixed-animal veterinary clinics: e-prescribe + DEA compliance + cattle herd records, replacing paper forms.
why: regulatory boundary IS the wedge (DEA + state vet board), narrow buyer
rag_context:
- AVMA estimates ~5,400 rural mixed-animal practices in US; AVImark/Cornerstone (1990s software) hold ~70% but lack DEA EPCS. (source: AVMA Practice Management 2024)
- DEA EPCS mandate for controlled-substance e-prescribing extended to veterinary in 2023; 60%+ of rural clinics still on paper. (source: DEA Diversion Control Division 2024)
- ACVS panel: rural vets pay $3,000-8,000/clinic ACV for SaaS that replaces paper + handles DEA. (source: ACVS Practice Trends 2025)
- gecko_precedent: 'vertical SaaS for regulated rural professional segment' SHIPPED 4 times in 2025-2026.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: Vertical SaaS for rural mixed-animal vets with DEA EPCS + herd records
Vet-tele-Rx replacing paper forms for small-fleet veterinary practices

text: An open-source 'design doc reviewer' that ingests an RFC PR + the repo and returns architectural critique grounded in actual file paths and prior PR decisions.
why: repo-grounded RAG over a real artifact, bounded scope
rag_context:
- Engineers spend median 4.2 hrs reviewing each RFC at large companies; 38% of comments cite 'lack of context vs prior decisions.' (source: Stack Overflow Dev Survey 2024)
- Sourcegraph Cody and GitHub Copilot Workspace target adjacent territory but don't ground reviews in prior PR decisions. (source: Sourcegraph 2024 product update)
- twit_sh: @rauchg, @leeerob, @sebmck publicly described 'PR reviewer that knows our repo history' as missing tool. (source: twit_sh 2025-2026)
- gecko_precedent: 'repo-grounded RAG dev tool' SHIPPED 3 times in 2025-2026.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: Open-source RFC/design-doc reviewer grounded in repo history + prior PRs
Dev tool that critiques PRs with citations to repo file paths and PR decisions

text: B2B tool for oncology trial coordinators: parses physician referral letters into trial-eligibility checklists keyed to ClinicalTrials.gov criteria.
why: narrow buyer, ClinicalTrials.gov is structured public data, automates manual task
rag_context:
- ~6,200 active US oncology trials; coordinators screen 30-80 referral letters per week, manual eligibility-checklist generation takes ~25 min each. (source: ASCO Clinical Trial Coordinator workflow study 2024)
- ClinicalTrials.gov XML API is free and structured (NCT IDs, eligibility blocks). (source: NLM ClinicalTrials.gov data dictionary)
- Trial coordinator software market: Forte, Veeva CTMS dominant but neither parses unstructured referrals. (source: Veeva 2024 CTMS market review)
- gecko_precedent: 'B2B oncology trial coordinator tooling' SHIPPED 2026-01-30.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: Oncology trial coordinator tool parsing referral letters to NCT eligibility checklists
Clinical trial screener SaaS for cancer center coordinators

text: A CLI that replays Stripe webhook payloads from a captured event log against a local server, with idempotency-key collision detection.
why: specific developer pain, Stripe CLI doesn't cover replay-from-prod
rag_context:
- Stripe CLI ships `stripe listen` + `stripe trigger` but does NOT replay captured production payloads. (source: Stripe CLI docs)
- Payments-eng teams pay $300-500/hr to debug webhook drift; 'replay prod webhooks against staging' is a recurring stack-overflow question. (source: Stack Overflow 'stripe webhook replay' tag, 2024-2025)
- twit_sh: @t3dotgg, @theprimeagen, multiple Stripe alumni asked for this exact tool publicly. (source: twit_sh 2025-2026)
- gecko_precedent: 'narrow Stripe-adjacent dev tool' SHIPPED 4 times in 2025-2026.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: CLI replaying Stripe webhook payloads from captured logs with idempotency check
Webhook replay-from-prod dev tool for payments engineers

text: MCP server that turns any Airtable base into a typed Pydantic schema and exposes safe CRUD tools to Claude Code with row-level diff preview.
why: ops/RevOps users on Airtable, MCP install zero hosting, typed schema is the moat
rag_context:
- Airtable has ~450k paid orgs (Series F filing 2024); RevOps + ops teams use it as their CRM/process backbone. (source: Airtable S-1 prep 2024)
- Anthropic MCP registry: official Airtable MCP exists but is untyped; community demand for typed/safe-CRUD variant is high. (source: anthropic.com/mcp issues 2025)
- twit_sh: ops-eng community publicly asked for 'typed Airtable MCP with diff preview' multiple times in Q1 2026. (source: twit_sh @meghan, @jess 2026)
- gecko_precedent: 'typed MCP server for SaaS data' SHIPPED 2026-02-15.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: MCP server exposing typed Airtable schema with safe CRUD + diff preview
Typed-schema MCP server for SaaS data tools (Airtable/Notion/HubSpot)

text: Carta-aware cap table diff tool: paste two SAFE term sheets, get a side-by-side dilution model with the founder/investor view toggleable.
why: founders + early-stage VCs, narrow doc class, calculation logic is the wedge
rag_context:
- Carta hosts ~40k cap tables; SAFE-vs-SAFE dilution math is a recurring pain point for founders fundraising; Carta's own UI lacks side-by-side diff. (source: Carta product gaps survey 2024)
- SAFE volumes Q4 2025: ~$2.1B raised on YC SAFEs alone; founders use ad-hoc spreadsheets to compare term sheets. (source: YC Demo Day data 2025)
- twit_sh: @paulg, @garrytan, @jaltma all referenced 'wish there was a SAFE diff tool' in 2025. (source: twit_sh 2025-2026)
- gecko_precedent: 'narrow doc-class workflow tool for founders/VCs' SHIPPED 3 times in 2025-2026.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: Cap-table diff tool for SAFE term sheets with founder/investor toggle
Dilution-model side-by-side comparator for early-stage VCs and founders

text: Part 107 commercial drone operator pre-flight checklist app with airspace + NOTAM lookup, $5/flight pay-as-you-go.
why: regulated buyer (FAA-licensed pilots), pay-per-use aligns with sporadic usage
rag_context:
- FAA reports ~390k Part 107 remote pilot certificate holders as of 2025. (source: FAA UAS Quarterly Report Q4 2025)
- B4UFLY (free FAA app) handles airspace check but does NOT generate per-flight checklists or store NOTAM acknowledgment for liability. (source: FAA B4UFLY product page)
- Drone pilot Reddit (r/dji, r/drones): pre-flight liability paperwork is the #1 complaint behind battery management. (source: r/drones 2025 mod-curated FAQ)
- gecko_precedent: 'regulated-pilot pay-per-flight SaaS' SHIPPED 2026-03-22.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: Part 107 pre-flight checklist app with airspace + NOTAM, pay-per-flight
FAA-compliance SaaS for commercial drone pilots

text: A web app that takes a research grant narrative + the funder's budget template (NSF, NIH, EU Horizon) and produces a compliant budget justification draft.
why: research admin pain real, funder templates change yearly, $200/grant willingness
rag_context:
- US universities filed ~80k NIH grant applications in FY 2024; budget-justification rewrite takes 6-12 hrs per submission and is the #1 sponsored-projects-office bottleneck. (source: NCURA Sponsored Projects Office Survey 2024)
- NIH PHS-398 form versions update annually; SF-424 budget tables differ across NIH/NSF/Horizon. (source: NIH grants.gov change log 2024-2025)
- Research admins polled at NCURA 2024 reported $150-300/grant willingness for an automated rewriter. (source: NCURA 2024 panel)
- gecko_precedent: 'grant/RFP narrative rewriter for research admins' SHIPPED 2026-02-28.

mock_precedents.idea_summary: Grant budget rewriter for NIH/NSF/Horizon templates targeting research admins
Compliant budget-justification draft generator for federal grant submissions
